subject: Product Management
title: The Cost of Not Experimenting
description: A quick win: understand The Cost of Not Experimenting.

hook.text: Kodak invented the digital camera in 1975 but shelved it to protect film sales.

buildup.text: Without experimentation, companies make assumptions about what customers want and what the market will tolerate.

discovery.text: The cost of not experimenting isn't just wasted development time — it's the opportunity cost of not discovering what works.

twist.text: Teams that don't experiment ship with false confidence. They celebrate launches without knowing whether the change helped or hurt.

climax.text: The real cost: teams that don't experiment learn slowly. Their competitors, running hundreds of tests per month, learn — and win.

punchline.text: Not experimenting isn't playing it safe. It's flying blind.

quiz.question: What is the biggest cost of not running experiments?
options: Teams learn slowly and ship changes without knowing if they helped or hurt — competitors who test learn faster
Experiments are always expensive to run
Not experimenting saves time and resources
